title: Rosa alpina intercalaris
intro: Rosa alpina intercalaris, also known as the Alpine Rose, is a species of rose that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and is prized for its unique and fragrant flowers. The Alpine Rose is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral activities, contributing to its potential in reducing stress, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Rosa alpina intercalaris include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and volatile oils, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in the form of capsules and essential oils, which are used to promote relaxation, improve sleep, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression.
taxonomy: Rosa alpina intercalaris is a specific cultivar of the alpine rose plant, belonging to the genus Rosa. It is a type of flowering plant that falls under the family Rosaceae. This family consists of deciduous and evergreen shrubs and trees, commonly known as the rose family. Rosa alpina intercalaris is a hybrid of Rosa alpina, a species of alpine rose native to the Alps, and another rose species. As a member of the rose family, it is a perennial plant that produces beautiful flowers and has a similar growth habit to other roses.
